title: proofbundle action-outcome predicate v0.1
description: External/docs schema for the vendored action-outcome/v0.1 predicate (Outcome Receipt, 3.2.0 core). The enforced validator is src/proofbundle/outcome.py (hand-rolled, fail-closed). An Outcome Receipt is signed by an EXECUTOR (a policy role distinct from the decisionMaker) attesting what was actually done, bound by digest to a Decision Receipt (decisionRef). lowerCamelCase (ITE-9). No-Overclaim: status=executed attests who signed what happened, never that the effect was good or desired.

relationDigest.description: relation/v0.1 content-root reference. digestAlgorithm is EXPLICIT and REQUIRED — never defaulted (a missing value is exactly where algorithm confusion hides).

relationships.description: relation/v0.1 (EXPERIMENTAL, 3.3.0): typed, SIGNED lineage edges to earlier receipts — inside the predicate, so the receipt's own signature covers them (unlike detached anchors). A verified edge proves the issuer DECLARED the relation over exact bytes: relationship declared by issuer, not a statement of correctness. The old receipt stays valid for its bytes forever.

decisionRef.description: content root: sha256 over the RFC-8785 canonical Statement bytes of the bound Decision Receipt (Stufe 2). Replay against a different decision fails.

requestedActionDigest.description: sha256 of the action that was requested (binds to the decision's proposedAction)

actualActionDigest.description: sha256 of the action actually performed

receiverRefs.description: Finding 16 (additive): third-party receiver/observer corroboration of this outcome, digest-bound exactly like a decision-receipt evidenceRefs[] entry.

sequence.description: Finding 16 (additive): an optional monotone (runId, seq) counter enabling detect_outcome_sequence_gaps to spot a suppressed outcome later in the same run.
